rationale: A complete WMI persistence triad was established by Administrator on host-01: an event filter (EV-001) triggers on notepad.exe process start, a CommandLineEventConsumer (A0) carries a heavily obfuscated PowerShell payload (no-profile, hidden window, base64+gzip encoded), and a filter-to-consumer binding (EV-002) links them — all created within milliseconds of each other. DER-001 confirms the decoded payload decompresses and executes an in-memory second-stage via IEX, a classic fileless execution pattern. EV-003 and EV-004 show the filter was subsequently modified (~6.5 hours later) to trigger on 'not-notepad.exe', suggesting the attacker adjusted the trigger condition, possibly to prevent repeated accidental firing or to evade detection — the subscription remains active. No evidence of cleanup or deletion is present, meaning the WMI subscription persists on the host.
